skill.name: enact-playwright
skill.description: Use Playwright to load web pages, capture screenshots, and extract text or HTML from CSS-selected elements.
skill.summary: Automates browser page capture and content extraction with Playwright.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: Static command findings in run.js are mostly false positives because they are JavaScript template literals used for logging. However, SKILL.md interpolates user-controlled inputs into a runtime command string, which may allow shell command injection depending on Enact execution semantics. The skill also intentionally performs arbitrary browser navigation, so publication should wait for safer argument handling and network-scope guidance.

high_findings.title: User-Controlled Command Argument Interpolation
high_findings.description: SKILL.md defines a runtime command that inserts url, action, and selector directly into a command string. If the runner executes this through a shell, a crafted value containing a single quote could escape the quoted argument and execute unintended shell syntax.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The manifest clearly interpolates user-controlled fields into a command string. Exploitability depends on the Enact runner's command parsing and shell execution behavior, which is not shown in these files.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary URL Browser Navigation
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts a user-provided URL and passes it to page.goto. In a networked execution environment, this can be used to request internal hosts, metadata endpoints, or private web applications that the caller could not otherwise reach.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL parameter is documented as user input and is passed directly to Playwright navigation. This is an expected feature, but it creates SSRF-style exposure in privileged networks.

medium_findings.title: Remote Page Content Extraction
medium_findings.description: The skill can extract text, HTML, or screenshots from the loaded page. This is intended behavior, but it can expose sensitive page content when used against authenticated, private, or internal URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The action enum and implementation directly support screenshot, HTML, and text extraction. The data exposure risk depends on what pages the execution environment can access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Template Literal Logging
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged run.js lines 16, 17, 18, 37, and 47 as shell backtick execution. These are JavaScript template literals passed to console.log, not command execution, although they can still log user-provided URL and selector values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is JavaScript and the flagged syntax is inside console.log calls. No child_process, exec, spawn, or shell evaluation appears at those lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Examples and Crypto Pattern
low_findings.description: The example.com URLs in SKILL.md are usage examples, not outbound calls by themselves. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at lines 4, 15, 18, and 26 have no supporting evidence because those lines describe browser automation fields and do not contain cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions and example commands only. No hash, cipher, digest, random generator, or cryptographic library usage is present in the referenced locations.

low_findings.title: Limited Filesystem Discovery for Browser Path
low_findings.description: run.js reads the /ms-playwright directory to locate the Chromium executable. This filesystem access is narrowly scoped and does not read user files, but it should still be documented because the skill performs local directory inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem calls are limited to existsSync and readdirSync on a fixed Playwright image path. No user-controlled path or broad filesystem traversal appears in the inspected code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell-Sensitive User Input in Command String
dangerous_patterns.description: The command field combines user-controlled parameters with shell-style quoting. Use argv arrays, structured argument passing, or strict escaping before publishing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command string contains direct placeholders for user input. The risk is high if the platform evaluates the command through a shell.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted Browser Navigation
dangerous_patterns.description: The implementation navigates to caller-supplied URLs without allowlists, scheme restrictions, or private network blocking.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code passes the supplied URL directly to page.goto. No validation or network policy is visible in the scanned files.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast browser checks without writing a custom script. This skill opens a page, captures screenshots, and extracts text or HTML from selected elements.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to a supplied web page URL in headless Chromium.
Capture a full-page screenshot and return it as encoded image data.
Extract text content from the full page or a CSS-selected element.
Extract inner HTML from the full page or a CSS-selected element.
Use Playwright Core with a Chromium browser from the Playwright container image.
limitations: It does not perform multi-step browser workflows or form submissions.
It does not validate whether a URL points to a public or private network.
It does not save screenshots as files by itself.
It relies on the available Chromium installation inside the runtime image.

use_cases.title: Check a Public Page Render
use_cases.description: Capture a screenshot of a deployed page to verify layout, visible content, and page load behavior.

use_cases.title: Extract Page Copy for Review
use_cases.description: Pull text from a landing page, documentation page, or selected element for review in an AI workflow.

use_cases.title: Inspect Generated Markup
use_cases.description: Extract inner HTML from a specific selector to compare rendered structure with expected output.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Page Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the public page I provide and extract the main text from the body element.

prompt_templates.title: Screenshot a Deployed Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Take a full-page screenshot of this public URL so I can review the rendered layout.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Specific Element
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this page and extract the text from the CSS selector I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Rendered Markup
prompt_templates.prompt: Load this public test page, extract the inner HTML for the target selector, and summarize structural differences from my expected component output.

output_examples.input: Extract body text from a public documentation page.
output_examples.output: The result contains the visible text from the page body, trimmed for direct review.

output_examples.input: Capture a screenshot of a public staging page.
output_examples.output: The result contains a full-page screenshot payload with start and end markers for downstream rendering.

output_examples.input: Extract inner HTML from a heading or content container.
output_examples.output: The result contains the selected element markup so a reviewer can inspect rendered structure.

best_practices: Use public URLs or controlled test environments unless the runtime is isolated from private networks.
Provide a narrow CSS selector when you only need one element.
Use screenshot mode for layout checks and text mode for content review.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to browse internal services from a privileged network.
Do not pass untrusted selector or URL values until command argument handling is hardened.
Do not rely on it for complex workflows that require clicks, authentication, or stateful browser sessions.

faq.question: What browser does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses Playwright Core with Chromium from the Microsoft Playwright container image.

faq.question: Can it take screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It can capture a full-page screenshot and return encoded image data.

faq.question: Can it extract only one part of a page?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a CSS selector to target a specific element for text or HTML extraction.

faq.question: Does it crawl multiple pages?
faq.answer: No. The implementation loads one URL per run and performs one selected action.

faq.question: Is it safe for private network URLs?
faq.answer: Use caution. The current implementation does not block private network destinations.

faq.question: Why is publication not recommended yet?
faq.answer: The manifest interpolates user inputs into a command string and needs safer argument handling before marketplace release.
